title: anthurium supraglandulum
intro: Anthurium supraglandulum, also known as the 'Queen of the Andes', is a flowering plant species within the anthurium genus. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with anthurium supraglandulum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often used to create infusions, teas, or ointments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
